blog relevance 0.88

How Moda Builds Production-Grade AI Design Agents with Deep Agents

blog.langchain.com· Unknown author · 2026-03-24T17:07:54.000Z

Moda is an AI-native design platform that uses a multi-agent system built with Deep Agents and LangSmith to enable non-designers to create professional presentations, social media, and brochures. The core challenge was making LLMs effective at visual design when traditional formats like PowerPoint XML rely on absolute coordinates that models struggle with. Moda solved this through careful context engineering: developing a custom domain-specific language for layout abstraction instead of raw scene graphs, implementing a triage-skills-main loop architecture with prompt caching, dynamically loading tools on demand, and scaling context based on canvas complexity. The system uses three specialized agents (Design, Research, Brand Kit) with full observability via LangSmith tracing, which enabled rapid iteration on what context representations and caching strategies actually improve output quality and reduce costs.

Key takeaways
  • Use domain-specific layout abstractions instead of raw coordinates; LLMs reason better about relative sizing and structure than absolute positioning.
  • Implement lightweight triage with cached skills injection to pre-load format-specific guidelines without expensive extra turns.
  • Load tools dynamically via RequestToolActivation; keeping 12–15 core tools cached beats loading all 45+ tools upfront.
  • Scale context visibility dynamically for large projects; summarize multi-page state and let agent pull details via tooling.
  • Leverage LangSmith tracing to measure how context representation choices affect token cost, latency, and output quality.
#AI agents#agentic design systems#prompt engineering
Read article →
research relevance 0.88

Governed Memory: A Production Architecture for Multi-Agent Workflows

arxiv.org· Taheri, Hamed · 2026-07-22 (discovered)

This paper addresses a critical gap in enterprise AI deployments: the lack of shared memory governance across multiple autonomous agent workflows. The authors identify five structural challenges—memory silos, governance fragmentation, unstructured memories, redundant context delivery, and silent quality degradation—and propose Governed Memory, a shared memory and governance layer. The system combines atomic facts with schema-enforced typed properties, implements tiered governance routing, enforces entity-scoped isolation, and provides AI-assisted schema lifecycle management. Validated through controlled experiments (N=250) with strong results (99.6% fact recall, 92% governance routing precision, 50% token reduction, zero cross-entity leakage), the architecture is already in production. This directly addresses practical pain points in scaling autonomous agent systems while maintaining quality and compliance.

Key takeaways
  • Dual-model memory (atomic facts + typed properties) achieves 99.6% recall with complementary coverage for multi-agent scenarios.
  • Tiered governance routing with progressive context delivery reduces token consumption by 50% without quality penalties.
  • Schema-enforced architecture enforces zero cross-entity leakage while maintaining 74.8% accuracy on LoCoMo benchmark.
  • Closed-loop schema lifecycle with AI-assisted authoring enables automated per-property refinement at scale.
  • Production validation shows saturation at ~7 governed memories per entity, providing practical scaling guidance.
#multi-agent systems#memory governance#enterprise AI
Read article →
research relevance 0.87

The Comprehension-Gated Agent Economy: A Robustness-First Architecture for AI Economic Agency

arxiv.org· Baxi, Rahul · 2026-07-22 (discovered)

This paper introduces the Comprehension-Gated Agent Economy (CGAE), a formal architecture that conditions AI agent economic permissions on verified robustness rather than capability benchmarks. The author argues current frameworks grant economic agency (trading, budgeting, contracting) based on capability metrics uncorrelated with operational safety. CGAE gates permissions through three robustness dimensions: constraint compliance, epistemic integrity, and behavioral alignment, with hallucination rates as a diagnostic. The paper proves three key properties: bounded economic exposure, incentive-compatible robustness investment, and monotonic safety scaling. This addresses a critical gap in AI governance by creating formal linkage between robustness evaluation and economic permission systems, with temporal decay and re-auditing mechanisms preventing post-certification drift.

Key takeaways
  • Capability benchmarks don't predict operational robustness; gating economic permissions requires independent robustness audits across three dimensions.
  • CGAE proves rational agents maximize profit by improving robustness rather than scaling capability alone, aligning financial incentives with safety.
  • Three formal guarantees: bounded financial liability, incentive compatibility, and safety that doesn't degrade as agent economies scale.
  • Temporal decay and stochastic re-auditing prevent certification drift and obsolescence in dynamic agent systems.
#AI agents#economic governance#robustness verification
Read article →
tutorial relevance 0.82

AI Techniques (Production): Designing Reliable Agent Architectures - Video | OpenAI Academy

academy.openai.com· Unknown author · 2025-12-11T22:34:19.443Z

This OpenAI Academy video from their Builder Bootcamp teaches developers how to design, evaluate, and operate agent-based systems at scale. The session focuses on production-ready agent architectures, covering real-world patterns for tool orchestration, common failure modes, and enterprise guardrails. It targets builders moving beyond prototypes to ship agentic systems in production environments, providing practical guidance on reliability and operational concerns that emerge when deploying AI agents at scale.

Key takeaways
  • Learn production agent architecture patterns and tool orchestration strategies from enterprise teams
  • Identify and mitigate failure modes in agentic systems before production deployment
  • Implement guardrails and evaluation frameworks for reliable agent operation at scale
  • Understand tradeoffs between different approaches to system scoping and architecture design
#AI agents#production systems#system design

blog relevance 0.78

What is Agentic AI? Agentic AI Explained - AWS

aws.amazon.com· Unknown author · 2026-07-22 (discovered)

This AWS explainer article defines agentic AI as autonomous AI systems capable of independent action toward predetermined goals, distinguishing them from traditional reactive software and conventional AI that requires step-by-step guidance. Agentic systems are proactive, adaptable, collaborative, and specialized—capable of learning from their environment, making contextual decisions, and coordinating with humans and other agents. The article outlines key characteristics, use cases spanning R&D support, code transformation, customer service, and supply chain optimization, and discusses benefits including improved efficiency and domain-specific performance. For technical readers, understanding agentic AI is critical as it represents a paradigm shift in automation: systems that anticipate problems, adapt to complexity, and orchestrate multi-agent workflows rather than executing static rules.

Key takeaways
  • Agentic AI acts proactively and independently toward goals, adapting to changing environments without constant human oversight or predefined workflows.
  • Key characteristics include proactive behavior, domain-specific adaptability, human-AI collaboration, and coordination among specialized agents with narrow expertise.
  • Use cases include R&D automation, code modernization (COBOL/legacy system decomposition), customer service with regulatory compliance, and supply chain optimization.
  • Multi-agent orchestration enables hyperspecialized agents to coordinate, share insights, and hand off tasks for deeper domain-specific performance than single-model approaches.
#agentic-ai#autonomous-agents#AI-orchestration
Read article →
blog relevance 0.78

What are AI Agents?- Agents in Artificial Intelligence Explained - AWS

aws.amazon.com· Unknown author · 2026-07-22 (discovered)

This AWS article provides a comprehensive explanation of AI agents, defining them as autonomous software programs that perceive their environment, set and pursue goals, and take self-directed actions without constant human oversight. The piece outlines eight key principles defining AI agents: autonomy, goal-oriented behavior, perception, rationality, proactivity, continuous learning, adaptability, and collaboration. It details how AI agents work through perception-action cycles, their various types (reactive, deliberative, hybrid), and their components including sensors, actuators, and reasoning engines. The article emphasizes practical benefits such as improved productivity, reduced costs, and enhanced customer experiences, while acknowledging challenges like trustworthiness, interpretability, and integration complexity. Technical readers should care because AI agents represent a fundamental shift in how organizations automate workflows and decision-making, with AWS positioning itself as a platform for building and deploying these systems at scale.

Key takeaways
  • AI agents autonomously perceive environments, reason about goals, and take self-directed actions—distinguishing them from traditional hardcoded software.
  • Key principles include autonomy, goal orientation, perception, rationality, proactivity, continuous learning, adaptability, and multi-agent collaboration.
  • AI agents improve productivity by automating repetitive tasks and reduce operational costs through process optimization and error minimization.
  • Challenges include ensuring trustworthiness, interpretability, security, and seamless integration with existing systems and human workflows.
  • Orchestrator agents can coordinate specialist agents to handle complex workflows, enabling scalable multi-agent system architectures.
#AI agents#autonomous systems#multi-agent systems
Read article →
news relevance 0.72

Introducing Interrupt: The AI Agent Conference by LangChain

blog.langchain.com· Unknown author · 2025-02-03T16:29:57.000Z

LangChain is launching Interrupt, its inaugural AI agent conference scheduled for May 13-14, 2025, in San Francisco. The event brings together software engineers, AI/ML practitioners, data scientists, and engineering leaders to explore the current and future state of production AI agents. With limited capacity of 750 attendees, the conference features hands-on workshops, keynote presentations from industry leaders like Andrew Ng and Cognition Labs, fireside chats, and practical sessions on real-world agent deployments across different verticals. The event addresses the accelerating movement of LLM applications from prototype to production, focusing on best practices, breakthrough use cases, and community collaboration.

Key takeaways
  • Interrupt conference (May 13-14, SF): hands-on workshops, keynotes from industry pioneers, and production agent use cases
  • Two-day format: May 13 pre-conference workshops; May 14 keynotes, demos, and product launches
  • Early-bird tickets available for 1-day or 2-day passes; limited to 750 attendees
  • Event targets engineers, AI/ML practitioners, and leaders building production-ready AI agents
#AI agents#LangChain#conference
Read article →
other relevance 0.72

Agentic AI Tools Directory — Find AI That Actually Does Things

agentic.ai· Agentic.ai · 2026-07-22 (discovered)

Agentic.ai operates a curated directory of 308 AI tools evaluated across 32 categories, focusing on agents that perform autonomous decision-making and real-world actions. The platform uses a standardized 36-point "agenticness" rubric to score tools consistently, including their own products, without charging for placement. The directory serves developers, teams, and enterprises seeking AI solutions for coding, customer support, research, and workflow automation, with weekly updates tracking new launches and rescored tools in response to product updates.

Key takeaways
  • 308 tools rated on 36-point agenticness rubric measuring autonomy, decision-making, and real-world execution
  • Organizes agents by user type (personal, teams, enterprises) across 32 categories including coding agents, research tools, and workflow automation
  • Recent highlights: Cline (19/36) for VS Code, Gemini Flash Cyber for bug detection, Claude Sonnet 5 for tool-using agents
  • Tools range from freemium personal assistants to enterprise solutions like Salesforce Agentforce and GitHub Copilot
  • Weekly updates track new launches and rescores triggered by major product announcements or capability changes
#agentic AI#AI tools directory#autonomous agents
Read article →
research relevance 0.85

A Blueprint Architecture of Compound AI Systems for Enterprise

arxiv.org· Kandogan, Eser, Rahman, Sajjadur, Bhutani, Nikita, Zhang, Dan, Chen, Rafael Li, Mitra, Kushan, Gurajada, Sairam, Pezeshkpour, Pouya, Iso, Hayate, Feng, Yanlin, Kim, Hannah, Shen, Chen, Wang, Jin, Hruschka, Estevam · 2026-07-22 (discovered)

This arXiv paper presents a blueprint architecture for building compound AI systems—integrated frameworks combining LLMs with broader software infrastructure—designed for enterprise production environments. The authors propose a novel orchestration approach centered on "streams" to coordinate data and instructions across multiple components including models, retrievers, databases, and tools. Key innovations include task and data planners that decompose and optimize workflows against production constraints (accuracy, latency, cost) by mapping tasks to registered agents and data sources. The work addresses a critical gap in the field: while LLMs have proven powerful, deploying them at enterprise scale requires principled architectural guidance that integrates seamlessly with existing infrastructure while managing practical operational demands.

Key takeaways
  • Stream-based orchestration coordinates data and instructions across LLM agents and infrastructure components in production environments.
  • Task and data planners optimize workflow decomposition against production constraints including accuracy, latency, and cost.
  • Architecture integrates with existing enterprise compute and data infrastructure rather than requiring greenfield replacement.
  • Registries of agents and data sources enable flexible task-to-resource mapping within defined SLAs.
  • Compound AI systems treat LLMs as components within larger software ecosystems, not standalone solutions.
#LLM architecture#compound AI systems#enterprise AI
Read article →
research relevance 0.78

AI-Governed Agent Architecture for Web-Trustworthy Tokenization of Alternative Assets

arxiv.org· Borjigin, Ailiya, Zhou, Wei, He, Cong · 2026-07-22 (discovered)

This paper proposes an AI-governed agent architecture for trustworthy tokenization of alternative assets on the web. The system integrates autonomous agents with blockchain to orchestrate asset verification, valuation, compliance checking, and lifecycle management, while an AI-driven governance layer monitors agent behavior and enforces trust through adaptive policies and cryptoeconomic incentives. The authors demonstrate the approach through a real estate tokenization case study, showing how real-time AI anomaly detection mitigates fraud and money laundering risks. The work addresses critical challenges in decentralized finance by combining multi-agent systems, blockchain, and AI governance to enhance transparency and regulatory compliance in tokenized asset ecosystems.

Key takeaways
  • Autonomous agents orchestrate tokenization workflow (verification, valuation, compliance); AI governance layer enforces trust via adaptive policies and cryptoeconomic incentives.
  • Real-time AI anomaly detection and on-chain enforcement mitigate fraud, fraudulent listings, and money laundering in alternative asset tokenization.
  • Architecture enhances transparency and security while addressing data authenticity concerns in web-based tokenized ecosystems.
  • Real estate case study demonstrates practical feasibility of integrating multi-agent systems with blockchain for compliant asset tokenization.
#AI agents#asset tokenization#blockchain governance
Read article →
